title: AWS Lambda MicroVMs target safer execution for AI-generated code
friendlyTitle: AWS adds safer mini-computers for running AI-written code
summary: AWS introduced Lambda MicroVMs, a new Lambda resource for running user- or AI-generated code in isolated, stateful Firecracker microVMs. The service is designed for fast launch and resume, per-session state, and stronger isolation than typical container-only approaches.
friendlySummary: AWS launched Lambda MicroVMs, which are small isolated environments for running code safely. They can keep state and start quickly.
whyItMatters: As coding agents and app builders increasingly execute untrusted code, sandboxing becomes core infrastructure rather than a security afterthought. This gives teams building AI coding tools, notebooks, scanners, and interactive environments a managed way to isolate risky workloads.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This helps companies test or run code made by users or AI agents without giving that code too much access. Safer sandbox, fewer dragons.
